proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_001 (3-level categorical, modal value 'former').
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_006='squamous' than in those with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
Mean pfs_months decreases monotonically across increasing values of feature_014 (ordinal 0/1/2).

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across feature_001 levels: mean pfs_months is 3.69 for former, 2.99 for current, 3.67 for never; F-test p<1e-200. Current smokers have markedly lower PFS.
Mean pfs_months: squamous=2.79, adenocarcinoma=3.68; t-test p<1e-200. Squamous histology has substantially shorter PFS.
Mean pfs_months: feature_014=0 ->4.30, =1 ->3.18, =2 ->2.12. Linear regression coef = -1.10 per unit (p<1e-200). Strong monotonic decrease consistent with ordinal performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 (binary, prev 0.65) is a strong negative prognostic factor: mean pfs_months is lower in patients with feature_023=1 than feature_023=0.
feature_011 (binary, prev 0.25) is associated with shorter pfs_months when =1.
feature_028 (rare binary, prev 0.05) is associated with shorter pfs_months when =1.
feature_016 (binary, prev 0.13) is associated with longer pfs_months when =1.
feature_022 (binary, prev 0.13) shows essentially no main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_023=0 -> 4.36, =1 -> 2.93; difference -1.44, t-test p<1e-200. feature_023=1 is a major adverse prognostic marker.
Mean pfs_months: feature_011=0 -> 3.66, =1 -> 2.74; difference -0.92, t-test p<1e-200.
Mean pfs_months: feature_028=0 -> 3.47, =1 -> 2.66; difference -0.81, p=1.4e-71.
Mean pfs_months: feature_016=0 -> 3.33, =1 -> 4.11; difference +0.78, p<1e-200. feature_016=1 has favorable PFS overall.
Mean pfs_months: feature_022=0 -> 3.41, =1 -> 3.60; difference +0.19, p=2e-11. Small positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_025 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5; likely albumin) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_017 (continuous, range 0-28) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_032 (continuous, range 50-829) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.769, p<1e-200. feature_015 is the strongest continuous correlate of pfs_months in the dataset.
Spearman rho = +0.100, p=6.3e-111. Higher feature_025 (likely albumin) tracks longer PFS.
Spearman rho = -0.125, p=1.8e-172. Higher feature_017 associated with shorter PFS.
Spearman rho = -0.014, p=0.0022. Statistically significant but very small correlation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, feature_014 (PS), feature_023, feature_011, hist_squamous, smoke_current, feature_031, feature_017, and feature_005 remain independently associated with shorter pfs_months, while feature_015, feature_025, feature_016, and feature_018 remain associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + feature_031 + smoke_current + smoke_former + feature_014 + hist_squamous + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_022 + feature_016 + feature_028 + feature_002 + feature_005 + feature_019 + feature_021 + feature_025 + feature_032 + feature_017 + feature_024 + feature_010 + feature_012 + feature_018 + feature_020 + feature_027 + feature_003 + feature_030 + feature_008 + feature_004 + feature_013 + feature_026 + feature_029 + feature_007 + feature_033 + feature_009', data=df2).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Independently significant predictors of pfs_months (linear regression, n=50000): feature_015 (+0.168/u), feature_014 (-1.10/u), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), hist_squamous (-0.72), smoke_current (-0.55), feature_031 (-0.20), feature_017 (-0.073/u), feature_005 (-0.11), feature_032 (-0.0004/u); positive: feature_016 (+0.89), feature_018 (+0.32), feature_025 (+0.46/u). Other features (feature_022, feature_028, feature_002, feature_019, feature_021, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, smoke_former, lab values feature_003/008/030/004/013/026/029/007/033/009/010/024) are non-significant in the adjusted main-effects model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features near 50% prevalence, feature_012 (50.0%) is a candidate randomized-treatment indicator and should show a meaningful main effect on pfs_months.
feature_018 (35% prevalence) is a candidate treatment with a positive main effect on pfs_months.
feature_021 (28.5% prevalence) is a candidate treatment with a (negative) main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_012=0 -> 3.449, =1 -> 3.414; difference -0.035, p=0.081. Essentially no main effect for feature_012; either not a treatment, or its effect is heterogeneous.
Mean pfs_months: feature_018=0 -> 3.319, =1 -> 3.641; difference +0.322, p=1.6e-53. Substantial favorable main effect — strongest treatment-like signal.

Mean pfs_months: feature_021=0 -> 3.486, =1 -> 3.296; difference -0.190, p=2.3e-19. Small negative main effect. After multivariable adjustment, feature_021 effect attenuates to non-significant (-0.005, p=0.47), so unlikely to be a primary therapy of interest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable main effect of feature_018 on pfs_months is preserved within strata of feature_006 (histology), with similar magnitude in adenocarcinoma and squamous patients.
feature_018 effect on pfs_months differs by feature_001 smoking status: current smokers benefit more than never smokers.

analyses.code: for v in df.feature_006.unique(): t-test feature_018 within stratum
analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma: PFS_018=0 ->3.57, =1 ->3.85, diff +0.28, p=7e-30. Squamous: 2.65 vs 3.10, diff +0.45, p=8e-32. Both histologies show favorable effect; squamous patients see slightly larger absolute benefit. Interaction f018*hist_squamous coef +0.171 (p=1.9e-4).

analyses.code: for v in df.feature_001.unique(): t-test feature_018 within stratum
analyses.result_summary: Former smokers: diff +0.30, p=1e-26. Current smokers: diff +0.51, p=4e-41. Never smokers: diff +0.014, p=0.78. Smoking is a strong effect modifier — never-smokers receive essentially no PFS benefit. Interaction f018*smoke_current coef +0.272 (p=1.7e-9).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 effect on pfs_months persists across feature_014 (PS) strata.
feature_018 effect on pfs_months is similar across feature_023 strata.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1,2]: t-test feature_018 within feature_014
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=0: diff +0.31 (p=1e-19). =1: +0.31 (p=1e-29). =2: +0.30 (p=2e-10). Effect is uniform across PS — no interaction. Regression interaction f018*feature_014 coef -0.007 (p=0.81).

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: t-test feature_018 within feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: diff +0.34 (p=6e-23). feature_023=1: +0.31 (p=3e-38). Effect not modified by feature_023. Interaction p=0.48.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 treatment effect is dramatically larger in patients with feature_016=1 than feature_016=0 (predictive biomarker pattern).

analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 stratum: feature_018 effect = -0.005 (n=43632, p=0.80). feature_016=1 stratum: feature_018 effect = +2.55 (n=6368, p=2.5e-275). Interaction coef +2.558, p<1e-300. feature_016 is a strong predictive biomarker — treatment benefit is essentially confined to feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 alone (without feature_018 treatment) is not a strong prognostic factor for pfs_months — the +0.78 main effect observed earlier is driven entirely by treated patients within the biomarker-positive group.

analyses.result_summary: Among untreated (feature_018=0): mean PFS for feature_016=0 ->3.334 vs feature_016=1 ->3.213; diff -0.12, p=4e-4. feature_016 is NOT prognostic in untreated patients (slightly negative). The apparent +0.78 main effect arises because feature_016=1 patients receive treatment and respond. Among treated (feature_018=1): feature_016=0 PFS=3.33 vs =1 PFS=5.77; diff +2.44.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1, the treatment effect of feature_018 is heterogeneous — feature_031 status (binary, ~45% prev) modifies it strongly.

analyses.code: Within feature_016==1: stratify by feature_031 and ttest feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1: feature_031=0 stratum (n=3508): feature_018 diff = +4.64 (PFS 3.23 -> 7.88, p<1e-300). feature_031=1 stratum (n=2860): diff = -0.012 (PFS 3.19 -> 3.18, p=0.88). feature_031 entirely abolishes the treatment effect within the biomarker-positive group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A formal three-way interaction feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 captures the heterogeneity: the f018*f016 effect (+4.66) is essentially canceled (-4.69) when feature_031=1.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way model: f018:f016 coef = +4.663 (p<1e-300); f018:f016:f031 coef = -4.688 (p=1e-300). f018 main = -0.019 (ns); f018:f031 coef = +0.032 (ns). Effect of treatment is concentrated in f016=1 AND f031=0 only — both conditions required.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 (the responsive subgroup), feature_028=1 suppresses the feature_018 treatment effect.
Within feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0, feature_005=1 suppresses the feature_018 treatment effect.
Within feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0, feature_022=1 partially attenuates the treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within f016=1 & f031=0: feature_028=0 (n=3370): feature_018 diff = +4.83 (p<1e-300). feature_028=1 (n=138): diff = -0.29, p=0.51. feature_028=1 abolishes treatment benefit (effect even slightly negative). Marginal interaction f018*f028 (across full cohort) coef = -0.49, p=4.1e-7.

analyses.code: Within f016=1, f031=0: stratify f005 and ttest f018
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=0 (n=3401): diff = +4.80 (p<1e-300). feature_005=1 (n=107): diff = -0.07 (p=0.88). feature_005=1 abolishes the benefit. Marginal interaction f018*f005 coef = -0.334, p=0.006.
Marginal interaction f018*f022 coef = -0.241, p=9.9e-5. Within f016=1 & f031=0: feature_022=0 (n=3284): diff +4.67. feature_022=1 (n=224): diff +4.36. Small attenuation, not full suppression.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup definition for treatment-effect concentration is feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; in this subgroup the feature_018 effect on pfs_months exceeds +4.5 months while in the complement it is essentially zero.

analyses.code: mask = (f016==1)&(f031==0)&(f028==0)&(f005==0); ttest f018 within mask and complement
analyses.result_summary: Defined subgroup (n=3266, 6.5% of cohort): PFS_018=0=3.249, =1=8.234, diff +4.99, p<1e-300. Complement (n=46734): PFS_018=0=3.323, =1=3.317, diff -0.006, p=0.76. Interaction subgroup*f018 coef = +4.99 (p<1e-300). The four-feature definition cleanly partitions responders from non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 main effect on pfs_months attenuates further in feature_016=1 & feature_031=1 patients — confirming the treatment is not merely modified but switched off in this stratum.
Within f016=1 & f031=1, no further binary feature reactivates feature_018 efficacy.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_016==1 and feature_031==1') and ttest f018
analyses.result_summary: f016=1, f031=1 (n=2860): mean PFS_018=0=3.191 vs =1=3.179, diff -0.012, p=0.88. Treatment is fully neutralized — direction even slightly negative.

analyses.code: Within f016==1 & f031==1: scan all binary features for f018 stratified ttest
analyses.result_summary: All within-stratum t-tests show diff between -0.50 and +0.24 with p>0.19; no other variable rescues a significant treatment effect. The f031=1 suppression appears global within the biomarker-positive group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous biomarkers feature_019, feature_025, feature_017, feature_032, feature_024, feature_010 do not meaningfully modify the feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in continuous_cols: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 * c', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All 17 continuous-feature interactions with feature_018 yielded |interaction coef|<0.10 and the only nominally significant one was feature_015 (coef +0.0035, p=0.010). Median-split stratified t-tests gave virtually identical f018 effects in low vs high halves (range +0.28 to +0.37). Continuous features are not relevant treatment-effect modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup (f016=1 & f031=0 & f028=0 & f005=0), additional features (feature_022, feature_002, feature_011, feature_023, feature_014, feature_001 smoking, feature_006 histology) do not further modify the very large feature_018 effect.

analyses.code: Within full subgroup mask: stratify all features and ttest f018
analyses.result_summary: Within the four-feature-defined subgroup, the f018 effect ranges from approximately +4.36 to +4.83 across all binary feature levels and from +3.98 (never-smokers, small n) to +4.78 (former). No additional modifier reaches |effect change| > 1 month relative to overall +4.99.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among never-smokers (feature_001='never'), feature_018 has no PFS benefit — suggesting feature_001='never' is a fourth co-suppressor.

Within f016=1 & f031=0, the absence of treatment benefit observed in never-smokers actually reflects the small never-smoker sample size in that subgroup; the feature_018 effect remains large within feature_016=1 & f031=0 even among never-smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Never-smokers (n=7459): PFS_018=0=3.670, =1=3.684, diff +0.014, p=0.78. Apparently no benefit overall.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_016==1 and feature_031==0 and feature_001=="never"'): ttest f018
analyses.result_summary: Within f016=1 & f031=0 & never-smoker (n=84): PFS_018=0=3.0 vs =1=6.98 approx, diff +3.98, p=2.4e-5. Therefore the never-smoker apparent null effect at the cohort level is driven by the very low prevalence of feature_016=1 & f031=0 among never-smokers; once you condition on the responsive subgroup, never-smokers still benefit (slightly less than smokers but materially).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 does not interact with feature_016 — it does not act as a treatment whose effect is concentrated in feature_016=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef f021*f016 = +0.026, p=0.78. Within feature_016=0: f021 effect = -0.22 (p<1e-20). Within feature_016=1: f021 effect = -0.18 (p=2e-3). feature_021 is a small global negative — not predictive-biomarker driven.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (50% prevalence, presumed randomized variable) shows no differential effect across feature_016 strata.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef f012*f016 = +0.083, p=0.51. feature_012 effect in f016=0: -0.020 (p=0.31); in f016=1: -0.134 (p=0.06). feature_012 is not a treatment with a predictive biomarker — likely a non-treatment indicator (e.g., a study-arm balance variable or non-clinical feature).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Although feature_022 modestly attenuates feature_018 effect (h27), it is not part of the cleanest predictive subgroup definition — adding f022=0 to the four-feature subgroup does not materially change either the within-subgroup effect or the complement effect.

analyses.code: Compare subgroup with vs without f022=0 added
analyses.result_summary: Four-feature subgroup (f016=1 & f031=0 & f028=0 & f005=0; n=3266): f018 diff +4.99. Five-feature subgroup adding f022=0 (n=3092): diff +5.04. Marginal change of +0.05 months at the cost of dropping 174 patients; f022 is not necessary for the subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is independently prognostic but does NOT modify the feature_018 treatment effect — its main effect persists in the multivariable model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 * feature_023 + feature_016', data=df).fit() and review
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef f018*f023 = -0.030, p=0.48 (no interaction). feature_023 main effect after adjustment for f018, f016 remains -1.42 (p<1e-200). feature_023 is purely prognostic, not predictive of feature_018 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong continuous correlate feature_015 (Spearman rho 0.77) is the dominant determinant of pfs_months; even after including it, the feature_018 x defined-subgroup interaction remains highly significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + feature_014 + feature_023 + feature_011 + hist_squamous + smoke_current + feature_031 + feature_016 + feature_018 + neg_subgroup_member + feature_018:neg_subgroup_member', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for feature_015 and other prognostic factors, the feature_018:subgroup interaction remains coef ~+5.0 with p<1e-300. feature_015 contributes most of the explained variance for the prognostic model (R^2 jumps from ~0.4 to ~0.7 with f015 included), but the predictive interaction is independent of it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis for treatment-effect heterogeneity in this cohort: pfs_months is increased by approximately 5 months on feature_018 only in patients defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0.
In the complement subgroup (any of feature_016=0 OR feature_031=1 OR feature_028=1 OR feature_005=1), feature_018 has no detectable effect on pfs_months (point estimate within ±0.01 month).

analyses.result_summary: Defined subgroup (n=3266; 6.53% of cohort): f018=0 PFS=3.25, f018=1 PFS=8.23, diff +4.99 months, p<1e-300. Complement (n=46734): f018=0 PFS=3.323, f018=1 PFS=3.317, diff -0.006, p=0.76. The four-condition rule is the cleanest known partition of treatment responders vs non-responders in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients in the defined responsive subgroup, the magnitude of treatment effect (~5 months) is preserved across performance status (feature_014), histology (feature_006), and disease-burden marker feature_023 — supporting that the predictive rule is biomarker-driven rather than confounded by prognostic factors.

analyses.code: Within neg_subgroup_member==1: stratify f014, f006, f023 and ttest f018
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: PS=0 diff +4.78, PS=1 diff +5.06, PS=2 diff +4.92; adeno diff +4.91, squamous diff +5.13; f023=0 diff +5.05, f023=1 diff +4.96. Effect is uniformly ~5 months across these prognostic strata, confirming biomarker-driven (not confounded) heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 represents the only treatment in this dataset with detectable treatment-effect heterogeneity on pfs_months; no other binary or continuous variable demonstrates a comparable magnitude or pattern.

analyses.code: Search all binary features: pfs ~ X * feature_016 and pfs ~ X * feature_031 etc., compare to feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Binary features feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027 yielded interaction coefficients with feature_016 of magnitude <0.15 with p>0.05 (no predictive pattern). feature_018 is the unique variable in this dataset whose effect concentrates in a clinically interpretable biomarker subgroup. Supports the conclusion that the only usable treatment variation in pfs_months is feature_018, with the four-condition responsive subgroup defined above.
